980	- no claims are created by this pass
981	
982	Suggested tests:
983	
984	- schema typecheck
985	- prompt schema parse smoke
986	- source ref validator smoke
987	- local fixture document outline smoke with mock adapter if available
988	
989	### Phase 2: Inject Outline into Document Extraction
990	
991	Deliverables:
992	
993	- `ensureSourceOutlineForDocument()` in document ingestion
994	- extraction prompt includes outline as `semi_stable_domain_context`
995	- extraction batches record outline ID in context metadata
996	- feature flag `enable_source_outline_pass`
997	
998	Acceptance criteria:
999	
1000	- document ingestion still succeeds when outline generation fails if feature is
1001	  configured as non-blocking
1002	- extracted claims still validate exact quotes
1003	- context packs show the outline block
1004	- disabling the feature returns to current behavior
1005	
1006	Suggested tests:
1007	
1008	- document ingestion typecheck
1009	- a fixture where pronoun/acronym resolution improves
1010	- quote validation regression: outline text cannot be cited as evidence
1011	
1012	### Phase 3: Meaning-Based Source Groups and Lens Extraction
1013	
1014	Deliverables:
1015	
1016	- `buildDocumentExtractionUnits()` prefers source groups
1017	- lens prompt addendum
1018	- batch metadata includes lens and source group ID
1019	- settings for enabled lenses
1020	
1021	Acceptance criteria:
1022	
1023	- the system can run handoff and exception lens passes
1024	- every lens output still enters the same candidate validation path
1025	- duplicate detection prevents obvious repeated claims across lenses
1026	
1027	Suggested tests:
1028	
1029	- fixture with handoff hidden across sections
1030	- fixture with exception branch in later chunk
1031	- duplicate candidate hash regression
1032	
1033	### Phase 4: Macro Relationship Extraction
1034	
1035	Deliverables:
1036	
1037	- schema for `macro_relationships` and `macro_relationship_claims`
1038	- prompt/schema for macro relationships
1039	- worker
1040	- validators
1041	- admin review page
1042	
1043	Acceptance criteria:
1044	
1045	- model can propose relationships over validated document claims
1046	- invalid claim IDs are rejected
1047	- rejected/superseded support claims are rejected
1048	- pending macro relationships are visible in admin
1049	- approving a macro relationship requires all support claims to be approved
1050	
1051	Suggested tests:
1052	
1053	- validator unit tests
1054	- macro relationship prompt schema smoke
1055	- admin action typecheck
1056	- fixture where two atomic claims produce one dependency relationship
1057	
1058	### Phase 5: Coverage Audit and Gap Creation
1059	
1060	Deliverables:
1061	
1062	- `source_coverage_findings`
1063	- coverage audit prompt/schema
1064	- worker
1065	- admin document coverage tab
1066	- optional gap creation action
1067	
1068	Acceptance criteria:
1069	
1070	- the system identifies at least one missing stage/owner in a fixture
1071	- findings never become claims automatically
1072	- created gaps link back to coverage finding/source outline
1073	
1074	Suggested tests:
1075	
1076	- fixture with outline stage not represented by claims
1077	- validator rejects invented refs
1078	
1079	### Phase 6: Chat Thread State
1080	
1081	Deliverables:
1082	
1083	- source outline support for channel threads
1084	- rolling thread summary after message segment extraction
1085	- injection into future message extraction
1086	- feature flag
1087	
1088	Acceptance criteria:
1089	
1090	- follow-up segments receive prior thread state
1091	- prior thread state cannot be cited as evidence
1092	- no segment extraction is blocked by a failed summary update
1093	
1094	Suggested tests:
1095	
1096	- two-segment fixture where "them" resolves from earlier segment
1097	- quote validation rejects thread summary text
1098	
1099	### Phase 7: Brain Synthesis Integration
1100	
1101	Deliverables:
1102	
1103	- synthesis loads approved macro relationships
1104	- synthesis corpus includes macro relationships and supporting claim IDs
1105	- diff validator accepts approved macro relationship citations
1106	- Brain section UI can display macro relationship citations
1107	
1108	Acceptance criteria:
1109	
1110	- Brain synthesis can cite macro relationship IDs
1111	- unsupported macro IDs reject the synthesis
1112	- named-entity validation still works through support claims
1113	- sections with more than 200 claims are not silently limited to a flat top-200
1114	  input in the final design
1115	
1116	Suggested tests:
1117	
1118	- synthesis validator test with macro relationship citation
1119	- rejection test for unapproved relationship
1120	- regression: atomic-claim-only synthesis still works
1121	
1122	### Phase 8: Retrieval and Chat Use of Macro Relationships
1123	
1124	Deliverables:
1125	
1126	- macro relationship retrieval helper
1127	- chat route includes approved macro relationship context in a separate block
1128	- context packs record included macro IDs
1129	
1130	Acceptance criteria:
1131	
1132	- chat answers can use approved macro relationships
1133	- answers cite or reference underlying approved claims where needed
1134	- existing `searchWithRetrievalPlan` remains the endorsed atomic claim path
1135	
1136	Suggested tests:
1137	
1138	- retrieval helper unit smoke
1139	- chat context pack smoke
1140	- no macro relationship leakage when feature disabled
1141	
1142	## Rollout Plan
1143	
1144	1. Ship tables and source outline worker behind feature flags.
1145	2. Generate outlines manually for 3 to 5 known workflow documents.
1146	3. Compare extraction quality with and without outline injection.
1147	4. Enable outline injection for admin document uploads only.
1148	5. Add macro relationship proposals, review manually, no auto-approval.
1149	6. Add coverage audit, create gaps manually at first.
1150	7. Integrate approved macro relationships into synthesis.
1151	8. Only after reviewer trust is established, consider limited auto-approval for
1152	   low-risk macro relationships.
1153	
1154	## Quality Metrics
1155	
1156	Track before/after:
1157	
1158	- claim acceptance rate
1159	- quote validation failure rate
1160	- rejected claim rate by reason
1161	- number of handoff/dependency/exception claims per source
1162	- reviewer revisions per 100 claims
1163	- coverage findings per source
1164	- macro relationships approved/rejected
1165	- Brain synthesis validation failures
1166	- admin-reported "missed big picture" incidents
1167	- answer quality for broad process questions
1168	
1169	Useful evaluation fixtures:
1170	
1171	- a flowchart/diagram with branches
1172	- a multi-page SOP with acronyms and pronouns
1173	- a Teams transcript where problem and resolution are separated by hours
1174	- a business-process document with cross-department handoffs
1175	- a document where one section defines a term and later sections use shorthand
1176	
1177	## Risks and Mitigations
1178	
1179	Risk: first-pass outline pollutes atomic claims with unsupported assumptions.
1180	
1181	Mitigation:
1182	
1183	- label outline as context only
1184	- prohibit outline source IDs as evidence
1185	- keep exact quote validation unchanged
1186	- add tests where model tries to quote outline text
1187	
1188	Risk: macro relationships weaken provenance.
1189	
1190	Mitigation:
1191	
1192	- keep them in a separate table first
1193	- require support claim IDs
1194	- require support claims to be approved before macro approval
1195	- document the exception in `DECISIONS.md`
1196	
1197	Risk: cost and latency increase significantly.
1198	
1199	Mitigation:
1200	
1201	- feature flags
1202	- manual admin triggers first
1203	- use provider caching for long sources
1204	- run lens passes selectively
1205	- avoid immediate full backfill
1206	
1207	Risk: reviewer UI becomes overwhelming.
1208	
1209	Mitigation:
1210	
1211	- start with documents page outline display
1212	- separate macro review queue
1213	- group by source and relationship type
1214	- convert weak relationships into gaps instead of claims
1215	
1216	Risk: synthesis trusts macro relationships too much.
1217	
1218	Mitigation:
1219	
1220	- synthesis validator resolves macro relationship support claims
1221	- require paragraphs citing macro IDs to cite underlying claim IDs in structured
1222	  output or store the expansion server-side
1223	- keep rejection behavior unchanged
1224	
1225	Risk: duplicate claims increase because lens passes overlap.
1226	
1227	Mitigation:
1228	
1229	- candidate hash dedup remains active
1230	- lens metadata helps inspect overlap
1231	- add per-source duplicate reports
1232	
1233	## Open Questions
1234	
1235	1. Should macro relationships be a separate first-class artifact permanently,
1236	   or eventually become a claim type with claim-to-claim evidence?
1237	2. Should source outlines be generated automatically for every upload, or only
1238	   for documents above a size/complexity threshold?
1239	3. Which model should own macro passes: `general`, `synthesis`, or a new
1240	   `macro` auxiliary route?
1241	4. Should pending-review atomic claims be allowed as support for pending macro
1242	   relationships? Recommended: yes. For approved macro relationships:
1243	   approved support only.
1244	5. Should macro relationships be visible to employee chat before Brain
1245	   synthesis uses them? Recommended: no until admin approval is established.
1246	6. How should source groups interact with batch extraction mode?
1247	7. Do we need embeddings for source outlines and macro relationships in round 1?
1248	   Recommended: macro relationship embeddings yes before chat retrieval;
1249	   source outline embeddings optional.
1250	
1251	## First Slice Recommendation
1252	
1253	The first shippable slice should be deliberately small:
1254	
1255	1. Add `source_outlines`, `source_outline_source_refs`, `source_groups`, and
1256	   `source_group_items`.
1257	2. Add `source-outline` worker for documents only.
1258	3. Add a manual "Generate outline" action on Admin Documents.
1259	4. Show the outline in Admin Documents.
1260	5. Inject the outline into document extraction behind
1261	   `enable_source_outline_pass`.
1262	6. Prove with one fixture that outline text cannot be cited as evidence and
1263	   direct quote validation remains unchanged.
1264	
1265	Then add macro relationships as the second slice.
1266	
1267	This avoids changing the evidence model in the first release while still moving
1268	the pipeline from bottom-up only to macro-first local extraction.
1269

file.content:
# Macro Understanding Implementation Plan

Status: planning document. No implementation has landed yet.

## Executive Summary

The current Oracle extraction pipeline is evidence-safe but context-myopic. It
promotes atomic claims only after deterministic quote validation, which is the
right foundation for trust. The weakness is that the system often asks each
model call to reason from a bounded local window:

- Document ingestion parses a source, chunks it into `document_chunks`, then
  runs extraction over one or more 24k-character document windows.
- Message extraction groups pending same-channel messages into 60-minute
  conversation segments, with prior messages passed only as non-quotable
  carry-in.
- Brain synthesis later sees approved claims, but only the claims that already
  survived local extraction.

This creates "contextual myopia": the system can prove local fragments while
missing source-level purpose, process sequence, pronoun/acronym resolution,
cross-chunk relationships, long-running chat threads, and coverage gaps.

The target architecture is macro-first and macro-throughout:

```text
Source
  -> source outline / macro map
  -> meaning-based source groups
  -> lens-guided atomic extraction using macro context
  -> deterministic validation and promotion
  -> relationship pass over validated claims
  -> coverage and contradiction audit
  -> synthesis over claims plus approved macro relationships
```

The central rule is strict: first-pass macro output is guidance, not evidence.
It can help local extraction interpret the source, but it must not become
approved knowledge by itself. Durable macro knowledge must be created only after
the system can cite either quote-validated atomic claims or explicitly validated
source spans.

## Goals

1. Preserve The Oracle's trust contract: every important answer or Brain
   artifact remains traceable to messages, document chunks, or approved claims.
2. Give extraction windows source-level context before they extract atomic
   claims.
3. Add a bounded, auditable macro-claim class whose evidence is a set of
   approved or pending-review atomic claims.
4. Detect when local extraction missed important parts of the source.
5. Improve Brain synthesis so it receives process-aware clusters and macro
   relationships instead of a flat top-200 claim list.
6. Make the new behavior observable and reviewable in admin screens.

## Non-Goals

1. Do not bypass deterministic quote validation for ordinary atomic claims.
2. Do not let an LLM-generated source outline directly write approved claims.
3. Do not remove the existing candidate-before-claim staging pipeline.
4. Do not create a second, competing claim retrieval path.
5. Do not require a new infrastructure service. State stays in Postgres;
   workers stay in Trigger.dev; web stays in Vercel.

## Current-State Constraints

Relevant current surfaces:

- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
  - parses documents and images
  - runs image vision transcription before extraction
  - persists `document_chunks`
  - builds extraction windows with `buildDocumentChunkWindows`
  - validates document quotes against one chunk
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-extraction.ts`
  - selects pending message segments via `selectPendingConversations`
  - passes carry-in context as non-quotable context
  - runs the candidate-before-claim pipeline
- `packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts`
  - strongly favors small, reviewable operational claims
  - requires one source ID and one exact quote per extracted claim
- `packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/promote-candidate.ts`
  - rejects candidates with no validated evidence
  - inserts permanent `claims`, `claim_top_domains`, `claim_evidence`,
    `claim_entities`, and `claim_metadata`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/brain-synthesis.ts`
  - reads approved claims by domain
  - hard-limits the main domain read to 200 claims
  - validates generated Brain text against approved claim IDs and the entity
    registry

The first implementation should respect these boundaries rather than replacing
them wholesale.

## Target Concepts

### Source Outline

A source outline is a provisional macro reading of a source. It answers:

- What is this source?
- What business process or situation is it about?
- Which departments, roles, systems, customers, licensors, factories, and
  geographies appear?
- Which acronyms, pronouns, aliases, or shorthand terms need resolution?
- What stages, branches, handoffs, exceptions, or open issues are visible?
- Which source chunks or messages seem to belong together conceptually?
- Which extraction lenses should run against this source?

It is guidance, not truth. It is allowed to be wrong. Downstream validators must
not treat it as evidence.

### Source Group

A source group is a meaning-based subset of source material. Examples:

- chunks that describe the same workflow stage
- transcript messages that are part of the same incident even if hours apart
- all chunks about a specific exception branch
- all messages naming a particular customer, factory, or system

Source groups are used to build better extraction inputs than arbitrary windows.

### Atomic Claim

An atomic claim is the existing claim shape: a concrete operational statement
with one or more quote-validated evidence rows. Round 1 can keep one evidence
row per candidate. Future rounds can allow multiple direct quote evidence rows
per atomic claim when the summary explicitly combines multiple quoted facts.

### Macro Relationship

A macro relationship is source-level knowledge inferred from already validated
atomic claims. It should be represented as either:

- a new `macro_relationships` table, or
- a controlled claim subtype with claim-to-claim evidence.

Recommended: use a dedicated table first, then optionally promote selected
relationships into `claims` after the review flow is proven.

Examples:

- "Licensor approval gates the handoff from Design to the China factory lane."
- "The failed-QA branch loops back to sample revision before customer update."
- "The morning delay discussion and afternoon resolution refer to the same
  packaging-artwork blocker."

Evidence is a list of validated claim IDs, not a free-form quote.

### Coverage Finding

A coverage finding says the source outline expected something that the extracted
claims did not cover. It should usually create a gap, not a claim.

Examples:

- "The outline identifies a QA-fail branch, but no claim captures who owns it."
- "The source defines three approval stages; only stage 1 and stage 3 produced
  claims."
- "Pronoun 'them' appears to refer to the offshore team, but no claim captured
  that definition."

## Data Model Plan

### Phase 1 Tables

Add hand-written SQL migrations and Drizzle schema for these tables.

#### `source_outlines`

Purpose: durable, provisional macro context for a document or conversation
episode.

Suggested columns:

- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
- `source_type varchar(50) not null`
  - `document`
  - `channel_thread`
  - `meeting_transcript`
- `document_id uuid references documents(id)`
- `channel_id uuid references channels(id)`
- `meeting_transcript_id uuid`
- `status varchar(50) not null default 'provisional'`
  - `provisional`
  - `superseded`
  - `failed`
- `outline_version varchar(50) not null`
- `model_run_id uuid references model_runs(id)`
- `context_pack_id uuid references oracle_context_packs(id)`
- `source_hash varchar(64) not null`
- `outline_json jsonb not null`
- `summary text`
- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
- `updated_at timestamptz default now() not null`

Indexes:

- `(source_type, document_id, created_at desc)`
- `(source_type, channel_id, created_at desc)`
- unique partial latest index is optional; simpler first version can query newest
  non-failed row by `(source_type, source_id, created_at desc)`.

#### `source_outline_source_refs`

Purpose: map outline elements to raw source rows so the outline is inspectable.

Suggested columns:

- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id) on delete cascade`
- `ref_type varchar(50) not null`
  - `document_chunk`
  - `message`
  - `claim`
- `document_chunk_id uuid references document_chunks(id)`
- `message_id uuid references messages(id)`
- `claim_id uuid references claims(id)`
- `ref_role varchar(50)`
  - `defines_term`
  - `stage_evidence`
  - `handoff_evidence`
  - `exception_evidence`
  - `open_question_evidence`
- `metadata_json jsonb`

Indexes:

- `(source_outline_id)`
- `(document_chunk_id)`
- `(message_id)`
- `(claim_id)`

#### `source_groups`

Purpose: meaning-based extraction units produced by the outline pass.

Suggested columns:

- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id) on delete cascade`
- `group_type varchar(50) not null`
  - `workflow_stage`
  - `handoff`
  - `exception_branch`
  - `incident_thread`
  - `entity_context`
  - `open_question`
- `title text not null`
- `description text`
- `sort_order integer`
- `metadata_json jsonb`

#### `source_group_items`

Purpose: ordered membership of chunks/messages in a source group.

Suggested columns:

- `source_group_id uuid references source_groups(id) on delete cascade`
- `item_type varchar(50) not null`
- `document_chunk_id uuid references document_chunks(id)`
- `message_id uuid references messages(id)`
- `sort_order integer not null default 0`
- primary key can be `(source_group_id, item_type, document_chunk_id,
  message_id)` if implemented carefully; a surrogate `id` is simpler.

#### `macro_relationships`

Purpose: durable source-level relationships inferred from validated claims.

Suggested columns:

- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
- `relationship_type varchar(100) not null`
  - `dependency`
  - `handoff`
  - `sequence`
  - `exception_path`
  - `contradiction_or_tension`
  - `definition_resolution`
  - `coverage_gap`
- `summary text not null`
- `status varchar(50) not null default 'pending_review'`
  - `pending_review`
  - `approved`
  - `rejected`
  - `superseded`
- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id)`
- `document_id uuid references documents(id)`
- `channel_id uuid references channels(id)`
- `confidence_score integer not null`
- `impact_score integer not null`
- `metadata_json jsonb`
- `model_run_id uuid references model_runs(id)`
- `context_pack_id uuid references oracle_context_packs(id)`
- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
- `updated_at timestamptz default now() not null`

Indexes:

- `(status, created_at desc)`
- `(document_id)`
- `(channel_id)`
- `(relationship_type)`

#### `macro_relationship_claims`

Purpose: claim-level evidence for macro relationships.

Suggested columns:

- `macro_relationship_id uuid references macro_relationships(id) on delete cascade`
- `claim_id uuid references claims(id) on delete restrict`
- `support_role varchar(50) not null`
  - `premise`
  - `enables`
  - `blocks`
  - `contrasts`
  - `defines`
  - `resolves`
- `sort_order integer not null default 0`
- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
- primary key `(macro_relationship_id, claim_id, support_role)`

Validator rule: every linked claim must have `status in ('approved',
'pending_review')` when the relationship is created, and only `approved` linked
claims can support an `approved` macro relationship.

#### `source_coverage_findings`

Purpose: reviewable audit of missed macro coverage.

Suggested columns:

- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id)`
- `document_id uuid references documents(id)`
- `channel_id uuid references channels(id)`
- `finding_type varchar(100) not null`
  - `missing_stage`
  - `missing_owner`
  - `missing_branch`
  - `unresolved_reference`
  - `unrepresented_exception`
  - `low_claim_density`
  - `conflict_without_contradiction`
- `summary text not null`
- `suggested_question text`
- `related_claim_ids jsonb not null default '[]'::jsonb`
- `related_source_refs jsonb not null default '[]'::jsonb`
- `status varchar(50) not null default 'open'`
  - `open`
  - `converted_to_gap`
  - `dismissed`
  - `resolved`
- `created_gap_id uuid references gaps(id)`
- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`

### Phase 2 Schema Extensions

After the separate macro table proves useful, decide whether to add
claim-to-claim evidence to the permanent claim model:

- add `sourceType='claim'` to the evidence source enum, or replace the enum with
  a varchar plus CHECK
- add nullable `source_claim_id` to `claim_evidence` and
  `extraction_candidate_evidence`
- allow a candidate evidence row to validate by checking referenced claim IDs,
  not quote text

Do this only after documenting a new decision in `DECISIONS.md`.

Recommended decision name:

`D-macro-relationship-evidence`

Decision boundary:

- Atomic claims still need direct source quotes.
- Macro relationships may cite approved claim IDs as evidence.
- A macro relationship is never evidence for another macro relationship in
  round 1. This prevents unsupported inference chains.

## Prompt and Output Schemas

Create new prompt/schema files under `packages/ai/src/prompts/`:

- `source-outline.ts`
- `macro-relationship.ts`
- `coverage-audit.ts`

### Source Outline Schema

Core fields:

- `sourceSummary`
- `sourceKind`
- `businessPurpose`
- `primaryDomains`
- `actors`
- `systems`
- `customers`
- `licensors`
- `geographies`
- `terms`
  - `term`
  - `meaning`
  - `sourceRefs`
- `processStages`
  - `stageId`
  - `title`
  - `ownerRoleOrDepartment`
  - `inputs`
  - `actions`
  - `outputs`
  - `nextStageIds`
  - `sourceRefs`
- `handoffs`
  - `from`
  - `to`
  - `artifactOrDecision`
  - `condition`
  - `sourceRefs`
- `exceptions`
- `openQuestions`
- `recommendedExtractionLenses`
- `sourceGroups`
  - `groupType`
  - `title`
  - `sourceRefs`
  - `whyGrouped`

Important prompt rule:

The outline must say "unknown" when a source does not define a term or owner.
It must not invent missing process steps.

### Lens Extraction

Keep the existing extraction output schema for atomic claims in round 1, but add
a preface block that includes the source outline and the active lens.

Suggested lenses:

- `handoffs`
- `exceptions_and_workarounds`
- `ownership_and_roles`
- `dependencies_and_sequence`
- `systems_and_data_entry`
- `contradictions_and_tensions`
- `definitions_and_acronyms`
- `customer_or_licensor_risk`

Implementation rule:

Each lens pass still emits ordinary atomic candidates with direct exact quotes.
If the active window does not support a claim with one direct quote, the model
should emit a gap or leave it to the relationship pass.

### Macro Relationship Schema

Core fields:

- `relationshipType`
- `summary`
- `supportingClaimIds`
- `supportRoles`
- `whyThisIsMacro`
- `sourceOutlineElementIds`
- `impactScore`
- `confidenceScore`
- `reviewReason`
- `riskFlags`

Hard validation:

- `supportingClaimIds.length >= 2` for most relationship types.
- Every supporting claim ID must be from the supplied list.
- Approved macro relationships require all support claims to be approved.
- The summary may not introduce named entities absent from support claim
  summaries or the canonical entity registry.

### Coverage Audit Schema

Core fields:

- `findingType`
- `summary`
- `sourceOutlineElementId`
- `relatedSourceRefs`
- `relatedClaimIds`
- `suggestedQuestion`
- `severity`
- `recommendedAction`
  - `create_gap`
  - `rerun_lens`
  - `manual_review`
  - `ignore`

## Worker Plan

### New Worker: `source-outline`

Location:

`apps/workers/src/trigger/source-outline.ts`

Inputs:

- `{ documentId }`
- later: `{ channelId, before?, after? }`
- later: `{ meetingTranscriptId }`

Document flow:

1. Load `documents` row.
2. Load all `document_chunks` for the document, ordered by `chunk_index`.
3. Build a full-source prompt. If the source is too large, use the provider
   cache/file-backed path where available; otherwise run a map-reduce outline:
   per-window mini outlines followed by a merge outline.
4. Compile through `OracleAIClient` using the `general` auxiliary route first.
   Do not overload the extraction route with outline work.
5. Insert `oracle_context_packs`, `model_runs`, `model_run_usage_details`, and
   `model_run_attempts`.
6. Insert `source_outlines`, `source_outline_source_refs`, `source_groups`, and
   `source_group_items`.
7. Mark stale prior outlines for the same source as `superseded` if their
   `source_hash` differs.

Why use `general`:

The outline is reading and organizing, not enforcing extraction JSON. Keeping it
as an auxiliary route allows admins to select a strong long-context model
without changing extraction pools.

### Modify Worker: `document-ingestion`

Current order:

```text
parse
  -> chunk
  -> embed
  -> extract windows
  -> validate/promote
```

New order:

```text
parse
  -> chunk
  -> embed
  -> source outline
  -> source groups
  -> lens-guided extraction over groups/windows
  -> validate/promote
  -> macro relationship pass
  -> coverage audit
```

Implementation details:

1. After chunk insertion, call an internal `ensureSourceOutlineForDocument()`.
2. Include the latest non-failed outline as a `semi_stable_domain_context`
   prompt block in each extraction plan.
3. Replace or augment `buildDocumentChunkWindows()` with
   `buildDocumentExtractionUnits()`:
   - prefer `source_groups`
   - preserve the max prompt budget
   - include adjacent chunk context as non-evidentiary context when needed
   - ensure the active evidence chunks are clearly labeled and citeable
4. Add lens scheduling:
   - round 1: one normal extraction pass plus targeted `handoffs` and
     `exceptions_and_workarounds`
   - round 2: add more lenses behind settings
5. Keep quote validation unchanged for atomic claims.
6. After all atomic candidates are processed, trigger
   `macro-relationship-extraction` and `source-coverage-audit` for the
   document.

Idempotency:

- Use `source_hash` over full normalized source text plus outline prompt version.
- Use `extraction_batches.batchType` values such as:
  - `document_source_group`
  - `document_lens_group`
- Do not reprocess groups whose `(source_group_id, lens, source_hash,
  prompt_version)` already completed unless admin requests reevaluation.

### New Worker: `macro-relationship-extraction`

Location:

`apps/workers/src/trigger/macro-relationship-extraction.ts`

Inputs:

- `{ documentId }`
- `{ channelId, sourceOutlineId }`
- optional `{ sourceOutlineId }`

Flow:

1. Load the latest source outline.
2. Load claims created from this source:
   - document: `claim_evidence -> document_chunks -> documents`
   - chat: `claim_evidence -> messages -> channel_id`
3. Prefer `status in ('approved', 'pending_review')`.
4. Group by source group, process stage, domain, entity, and claim type.
5. Run `OracleAIClient.runObject` with the macro relationship schema.
6. Validate:
   - all support claim IDs exist
   - support claims belong to the source or allowed adjacent thread context
   - no support claim is rejected or superseded
   - if relationship status is `approved`, all support claims must be approved
   - summary named entities are backed by support claims or entity registry
7. Insert `macro_relationships` as `pending_review`.
8. Insert `macro_relationship_claims`.
9. Optionally auto-approve low-risk relationships only after there is admin
   review experience. Round 1 should not auto-approve.

### New Worker: `source-coverage-audit`

Location:

`apps/workers/src/trigger/source-coverage-audit.ts`

Inputs:

- `{ documentId }`
- `{ sourceOutlineId }`

Flow:

1. Load outline.
2. Load extracted atomic claims and macro relationships for the source.
3. Ask the model what outline elements are not represented.
4. Validate output references:
   - outline element IDs must exist
   - claim IDs must exist
   - source refs must belong to the source
5. Insert `source_coverage_findings`.
6. For `recommendedAction='create_gap'`, create `gaps` rows with a new
   `gap_type` such as `source_coverage_gap`, or reuse an existing operational
   gap type if it fits.

### Modify Worker: `claim-extraction`

Round 1 for chat:

1. Keep current 60-minute segments for atomic extraction.
2. Add `thread_state_summary` as a source outline variant for channel threads.
3. After each segment, update the latest channel outline with:
   - active entities
   - unresolved references
   - open operational problems
   - recent decisions
   - possible continuation markers
4. Pass the latest outline into future segment extraction as
   `semi_stable_domain_context`.
5. Do not let prior outline content be quoted or claimed directly.

Round 2 for chat:

1. Build long-running incident threads by clustering messages across time using:
   - same channel
   - shared entities
   - shared customer/licensor/system
   - reply metadata if available
   - semantic similarity over message embeddings if later added
2. Run macro relationship extraction over completed incident threads.

### Modify Worker: `brain-synthesis`

Round 1:

1. Load approved macro relationships relevant to the section's top domains.
2. Include them in the synthesis corpus after atomic claims:

```text
APPROVED MACRO RELATIONSHIPS:
- ID: ...
  Type: dependency
  Relationship: ...
  Supporting claims: ...
```

3. Extend synthesis validation so a paragraph may cite:
   - approved claim IDs, and/or
   - approved macro relationship IDs
4. Require macro relationship IDs to resolve to approved rows.
5. The validator should still inspect the underlying support claim summaries for
   named-entity backing.

Round 2:

1. Replace the flat top-200 read with staged subtopic/cluster batches:
   - high-impact claims
   - recently changed claims
   - approved macro relationships
   - taxonomy subtopics
   - explicit section bindings
2. Generate or refresh one subsection at a time.
3. Preserve existing rejected-version behavior on validation failure.

## Admin UI Plan

### Admin Documents

Enhance `apps/web/app/admin/documents`:

- show source outline status
- show model used for outline
- show outline summary
- show process stages and source groups
- add actions:
  - "Generate outline"
  - "Regenerate outline"
  - "Run macro relationship pass"
  - "Run coverage audit"
  - "Reevaluate document with macro context"

### Admin Claims

Round 1:

- show whether a claim was extracted with a source outline
- show source group/lens metadata if available

Round 2:

- add filter by source group, lens, or process stage
- show related macro relationships for each claim

### New Admin Page: Macro Review

Path:

`apps/web/app/admin/macro-relationships`

Features:

- pending review queue
- relationship summary
- source outline summary
- supporting claim cards with evidence quotes
- approve/reject/revise
- convert to gap
- links back to document/chunks/messages

Actions:

- approve macro relationship
- reject macro relationship
- revise summary
- create gap from relationship/finding

Audit:

- add `macro_relationship_review_events`, or use a generic review event table.
  Prefer a dedicated table if the UI needs before/after snapshots similar to
  `claim_review_events`.

### Admin Gaps

Show coverage findings that created gaps and link back to source outline.

## Retrieval and Chat Plan

Round 1:

- Do not change `searchWithRetrievalPlan`.
- Chat still retrieves approved atomic claims.
- Brain snippets remain as today.

Round 2:

- Add macro relationships to retrieval context as a separate retrieved block,
  not mixed into atomic claim RRF at first.
- Search macro relationships by:
  - embedded summary
  - linked support claim domains/entities
  - relationship type
- Include only approved macro relationships.
- In chat prompt, label them distinctly:

```text
APPROVED PROCESS RELATIONSHIPS
These are higher-level relationships supported by approved claim IDs.
```

This avoids contaminating the single endorsed claim retrieval path while adding
a second, explicitly typed retrieval stream.

## Validation Plan

### Atomic Claims

No weakening:

- source pointer must exist
- quote must validate against one active source message or one document chunk
- taxonomy must validate
- sensitivity gate still runs
- promotion still goes through `executePromotion`

### Source Outlines

Validation should check structure only:

- JSON schema valid
- source refs exist and belong to the source
- no invented chunk/message IDs
- maximum sizes respected

Outlines are allowed to contain uncertain language.

### Macro Relationships

Validation should check:

- all supporting claim IDs exist
- support claims are not rejected or superseded
- source scoping is correct
- relationship summary does not introduce unsupported named entities
- if status is `approved`, every support claim is approved
- no recursive support from another macro relationship in round 1
- no relationship with fewer than two support claims unless type is
  `definition_resolution` and it has a clear source outline ref

### Coverage Findings

Validation should check:

- referenced outline element exists
- source refs belong to the source
- suggested gap text is actionable
- finding does not assert a new operational fact as true

## Settings Plan

Add settings:

- `enable_source_outline_pass`
- `enable_macro_relationship_pass`
- `enable_source_coverage_audit`
- `macro_outline_max_chars`
- `macro_relationship_max_support_claims`
- `macro_lenses_enabled`
- `default_macro_route`

`default_macro_route` should be implemented as a new auxiliary model:

- add to `packages/ai/src/routes/auxiliary.ts`
- expose in Admin Settings
- seed with `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`

Alternative:

- reuse `default_general_purpose_route` initially.

Recommendation:

- Start by reusing `general` to keep migration small.
- Add `macro` auxiliary slot only if outline/relationship calls need different
  model economics than taxonomy naming and other general work.

## Observability Plan

Every new model call must write:

- `oracle_context_packs`
- `model_runs`
- `model_run_usage_details`
- `model_run_attempts`

Use task types:

- `source_outline`
- `macro_relationship_extraction`
- `source_coverage_audit`

Context pack observability:

- `includedDocumentChunkIds`
- `includedMessageIds`
- `includedClaimIds`
- `selectedDomains`
- source outline ID in `blocksJson` metadata or a future
  `includedSourceOutlineIds` field

Admin run summaries should show:

- outline generated or skipped
- groups created
- extraction units run
- candidates staged
- claims promoted
- macro relationships proposed
- coverage findings created
- failures by validation check

## Migration and Backfill Plan

### Migration 1

Add source outline tables and macro relationship tables.

Files:

- `packages/db/src/schema.ts`
- `packages/db/migrations/sql/79_macro_understanding.sql`

If generated Drizzle migration is desired later, keep the hand-written SQL
idempotent and reconcile drift intentionally.

### Migration 2

Add optional metadata fields to existing tables:

- `extraction_batches.batch_type` values are varchar already, so no schema
  change needed for new batch types.
- `documents` can remain unchanged if outlines live in `source_outlines`.
  Avoid adding `documents.global_context_json` because it will become cramped
  and hides version history.

### Backfill

1. Generate source outlines for the most important existing documents first.
2. Run macro relationship extraction on documents with:
   - many approved claims
   - known workflow/process content
   - known diagram/image ingestion issues
3. Do not backfill every document immediately. Cost and reviewer load will be
   high.

Backfill script:

- `scripts/backfill-source-outlines.mjs`
- dry-run by default
- filters by document ID, status, created date, minimum approved claims
- triggers tasks rather than doing provider work inline

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 0: Decision and Guardrails

Deliverables:

- add `DECISIONS.md` entry for macro understanding
- confirm evidence rule:
  - source outlines are guidance
  - atomic claims require direct quotes
  - macro relationships cite validated claim IDs
- add this plan to docs map

Acceptance criteria:

- future sessions can name the provenance boundary in one paragraph
- no code change yet mutates production behavior

### Phase 1: Source Outline Storage and Worker

Deliverables:

- schema and migration for `source_outlines`, refs, groups, group items
- `packages/ai/src/prompts/source-outline.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-outline.ts`
- admin action to generate outline for a document
- context pack/model run logging

Acceptance criteria:

- a document can produce one source outline
- outline source refs all validate against existing chunks
- admin can inspect the outline
- no claims are created by this pass

Suggested tests:

- schema typecheck
- prompt schema parse smoke
- source ref validator smoke
- local fixture document outline smoke with mock adapter if available

### Phase 2: Inject Outline into Document Extraction

Deliverables:

- `ensureSourceOutlineForDocument()` in document ingestion
- extraction prompt includes outline as `semi_stable_domain_context`
- extraction batches record outline ID in context metadata
- feature flag `enable_source_outline_pass`

Acceptance criteria:

- document ingestion still succeeds when outline generation fails if feature is
  configured as non-blocking
- extracted claims still validate exact quotes
- context packs show the outline block
- disabling the feature returns to current behavior

Suggested tests:

- document ingestion typecheck
- a fixture where pronoun/acronym resolution improves
- quote validation regression: outline text cannot be cited as evidence

### Phase 3: Meaning-Based Source Groups and Lens Extraction

Deliverables:

- `buildDocumentExtractionUnits()` prefers source groups
- lens prompt addendum
- batch metadata includes lens and source group ID
- settings for enabled lenses

Acceptance criteria:

- the system can run handoff and exception lens passes
- every lens output still enters the same candidate validation path
- duplicate detection prevents obvious repeated claims across lenses

Suggested tests:

- fixture with handoff hidden across sections
- fixture with exception branch in later chunk
- duplicate candidate hash regression

### Phase 4: Macro Relationship Extraction

Deliverables:

- schema for `macro_relationships` and `macro_relationship_claims`
- prompt/schema for macro relationships
- worker
- validators
- admin review page

Acceptance criteria:

- model can propose relationships over validated document claims
- invalid claim IDs are rejected
- rejected/superseded support claims are rejected
- pending macro relationships are visible in admin
- approving a macro relationship requires all support claims to be approved

Suggested tests:

- validator unit tests
- macro relationship prompt schema smoke
- admin action typecheck
- fixture where two atomic claims produce one dependency relationship

### Phase 5: Coverage Audit and Gap Creation

Deliverables:

- `source_coverage_findings`
- coverage audit prompt/schema
- worker
- admin document coverage tab
- optional gap creation action

Acceptance criteria:

- the system identifies at least one missing stage/owner in a fixture
- findings never become claims automatically
- created gaps link back to coverage finding/source outline

Suggested tests:

- fixture with outline stage not represented by claims
- validator rejects invented refs

### Phase 6: Chat Thread State

Deliverables:

- source outline support for channel threads
- rolling thread summary after message segment extraction
- injection into future message extraction
- feature flag

Acceptance criteria:

- follow-up segments receive prior thread state
- prior thread state cannot be cited as evidence
- no segment extraction is blocked by a failed summary update

Suggested tests:

- two-segment fixture where "them" resolves from earlier segment
- quote validation rejects thread summary text

### Phase 7: Brain Synthesis Integration

Deliverables:

- synthesis loads approved macro relationships
- synthesis corpus includes macro relationships and supporting claim IDs
- diff validator accepts approved macro relationship citations
- Brain section UI can display macro relationship citations

Acceptance criteria:

- Brain synthesis can cite macro relationship IDs
- unsupported macro IDs reject the synthesis
- named-entity validation still works through support claims
- sections with more than 200 claims are not silently limited to a flat top-200
  input in the final design

Suggested tests:

- synthesis validator test with macro relationship citation
- rejection test for unapproved relationship
- regression: atomic-claim-only synthesis still works

### Phase 8: Retrieval and Chat Use of Macro Relationships

Deliverables:

- macro relationship retrieval helper
- chat route includes approved macro relationship context in a separate block
- context packs record included macro IDs

Acceptance criteria:

- chat answers can use approved macro relationships
- answers cite or reference underlying approved claims where needed
- existing `searchWithRetrievalPlan` remains the endorsed atomic claim path

Suggested tests:

- retrieval helper unit smoke
- chat context pack smoke
- no macro relationship leakage when feature disabled

## Rollout Plan

1. Ship tables and source outline worker behind feature flags.
2. Generate outlines manually for 3 to 5 known workflow documents.
3. Compare extraction quality with and without outline injection.
4. Enable outline injection for admin document uploads only.
5. Add macro relationship proposals, review manually, no auto-approval.
6. Add coverage audit, create gaps manually at first.
7. Integrate approved macro relationships into synthesis.
8. Only after reviewer trust is established, consider limited auto-approval for
   low-risk macro relationships.

## Quality Metrics

Track before/after:

- claim acceptance rate
- quote validation failure rate
- rejected claim rate by reason
- number of handoff/dependency/exception claims per source
- reviewer revisions per 100 claims
- coverage findings per source
- macro relationships approved/rejected
- Brain synthesis validation failures
- admin-reported "missed big picture" incidents
- answer quality for broad process questions

Useful evaluation fixtures:

- a flowchart/diagram with branches
- a multi-page SOP with acronyms and pronouns
- a Teams transcript where problem and resolution are separated by hours
- a business-process document with cross-department handoffs
- a document where one section defines a term and later sections use shorthand

## Risks and Mitigations

Risk: first-pass outline pollutes atomic claims with unsupported assumptions.

Mitigation:

- label outline as context only
- prohibit outline source IDs as evidence
- keep exact quote validation unchanged
- add tests where model tries to quote outline text

Risk: macro relationships weaken provenance.

Mitigation:

- keep them in a separate table first
- require support claim IDs
- require support claims to be approved before macro approval
- document the exception in `DECISIONS.md`

Risk: cost and latency increase significantly.

Mitigation:

- feature flags
- manual admin triggers first
- use provider caching for long sources
- run lens passes selectively
- avoid immediate full backfill

Risk: reviewer UI becomes overwhelming.

Mitigation:

- start with documents page outline display
- separate macro review queue
- group by source and relationship type
- convert weak relationships into gaps instead of claims

Risk: synthesis trusts macro relationships too much.

Mitigation:

- synthesis validator resolves macro relationship support claims
- require paragraphs citing macro IDs to cite underlying claim IDs in structured
  output or store the expansion server-side
- keep rejection behavior unchanged

Risk: duplicate claims increase because lens passes overlap.

Mitigation:

- candidate hash dedup remains active
- lens metadata helps inspect overlap
- add per-source duplicate reports

## Open Questions

1. Should macro relationships be a separate first-class artifact permanently,
   or eventually become a claim type with claim-to-claim evidence?
2. Should source outlines be generated automatically for every upload, or only
   for documents above a size/complexity threshold?
3. Which model should own macro passes: `general`, `synthesis`, or a new
   `macro` auxiliary route?
4. Should pending-review atomic claims be allowed as support for pending macro
   relationships? Recommended: yes. For approved macro relationships:
   approved support only.
5. Should macro relationships be visible to employee chat before Brain
   synthesis uses them? Recommended: no until admin approval is established.
6. How should source groups interact with batch extraction mode?
7. Do we need embeddings for source outlines and macro relationships in round 1?
   Recommended: macro relationship embeddings yes before chat retrieval;
   source outline embeddings optional.

## First Slice Recommendation

The first shippable slice should be deliberately small:

1. Add `source_outlines`, `source_outline_source_refs`, `source_groups`, and
   `source_group_items`.
2. Add `source-outline` worker for documents only.
3. Add a manual "Generate outline" action on Admin Documents.
4. Show the outline in Admin Documents.
5. Inject the outline into document extraction behind
   `enable_source_outline_pass`.
6. Prove with one fixture that outline text cannot be cited as evidence and
   direct quote validation remains unchanged.

Then add macro relationships as the second slice.

This avoids changing the evidence model in the first release while still moving
the pipeline from bottom-up only to macro-first local extraction.

content.text:
Verified the two things the plan's trust story depends on. Here's the assessment.

## Does it have everything I recommended? Yes — and materially more.

Mapping my recommendations to the plan:

| My recommendation | In the plan? |
|---|---|
| Second pass over *validated* claims for cross-window relationships, evidence = set of claims | ✅ `macro_relationships` + `macro_relationship_claims`, evidence is `supportingClaimIds` |
| A first-pass context step (the "why not first?" exchange) — non-evidentiary priming | ✅ `source-outline` worker → `semi_stable_domain_context` injected into extraction |
| Tiered confidence, weak evidence never masquerades as strong | ✅ separate tables, separate retrieval block, distinctly labeled in chat/synthesis prompts |
| Tier-3 "inferred, unverified" routed into review/gaps rather than stored as fact | ✅ handled *better* than I proposed — as `source_coverage_findings` → gaps, so unverified inference becomes a **question**, not stored knowledge |
| Fix synthesis flat top-200 cap | ✅ Phase 7 acceptance: "sections with more than 200 claims are not silently limited to a flat top-200" |
| Keep the strict quote gate untouched | ✅ stated as a hard Non-Goal and in the validation plan |

Beyond what I said, it adds four things I did **not** propose and should have: **meaning-based source groups** (fixes arbitrary-window boundaries at the root, not just with a second pass), **lens-guided extraction** (targeted passes for handoffs/exceptions/ownership), **chat thread-state** (rolling summaries so 60-min segments stop being amnesiac), and a **coverage-audit critic** (the "what did we miss" pass). Plus full observability, phased rollout, feature flags, metrics, and eval fixtures. It's a real plan, not a sketch.

## What it misses or under-treats

These are genuine gaps, ordered by how much they threaten the plan's own stated goal (preserve the trust contract):

**1. Macro-relationship staleness when a support claim dies — a real provenance hole.** I confirmed: `claims.status` includes `superseded` ([schema.ts:97](packages/db/src/schema.ts:97)), and claim revision is supersede-and-replace (AGENTS.md §10). The plan validates support-claim status **only at creation and at approval** — there is no trigger or worker that re-opens/flags an *already-approved* macro relationship when one of its support claims is later superseded, revised, or rejected. So a Tier-2 artifact can silently rest on a dead claim. That's exactly the "weak evidence masquerading as strong" failure the whole design exists to prevent, just moved up a level. Fix: a watcher (or FK-driven flag) that flips affected macro relationships to `needs_review` when any support claim leaves `approved`. This is the macro analog of the recertification concern in `docs/oracle/07`.

**2. The macro pass runs before human approval, but approved-macro requires approved-support.** In the new `document-ingestion` order, `macro-relationship-extraction` fires "after all atomic candidates are processed" — i.e. when claims are mostly `pending_review`. But approval of a macro relationship requires all support claims approved, which happens later, asynchronously, via human review. So freshly-generated macro relationships will mostly sit stuck until their support claims are individually approved — with nothing described to **re-trigger or re-validate them after approval**. The lifecycle needs an approval-driven re-run, not just a post-extraction one.

**3. Cross-source is where the biggest "big picture" lives, and the macro pass is source-scoped.** Both rounds scope macro relationships to one `source_outline` (one document, or one channel thread). But the highest-value macro insight is usually cross-source: an SOP document says X, a Teams transcript shows people actually do Y. The plan leaves that entirely to Brain synthesis. Worth naming as an explicit future round, and it connects to the next point —

**4. `claim_kind` (policy vs observed_practice) is specified in doc 07 but not implemented — I checked, it's absent from the schema.** That taxonomy is exactly the vocabulary for the most valuable cross-source relationship ("official policy X, observed practice Y, workaround Z"). The plan's `contradiction_or_tension` relationship type gestures at it, but without `claim_kind` the model can't cleanly distinguish "these contradict" from "this is the sanctioned exception to that." Folding `claim_kind` in would make that relationship type far sharper.

**5. Overlap with the existing `contradiction-watcher`.** There's already a semantic contradiction pipeline writing to a `contradictions` table (architecture.md §7). The macro `contradiction_or_tension` type will find some of the same tensions through a different path, into a different table, surfaced in a different admin screen. The plan doesn't reconcile the two — you risk two systems, two queues, duplicate findings. Decide whether macro feeds the existing watcher or replaces part of it.

**6. Reviewer load is the real adoption risk, and it's under-treated relative to provenance risk.** The plan adds: more atomic claims (lens fan-out), macro relationships, and coverage findings — all `pending_review`, no auto-approval in round 1. Human review is already the system's throughput bottleneck (the whole graph gates on it). The mitigations listed are organizational ("group by type"), not prioritization. There's no impact/confidence-ranked triage so a reviewer sees the 5 that matter first. Without that, this plan can *stall the queue* even though every piece is individually correct.

**7. Dedup across lenses leans on `candidate_hash`, which won't catch it.** Eight lenses reading overlapping text will surface the same underlying fact with different wording/quote selection → different hashes → duplicates that hash-dedup misses. The plan lists this as a risk but the mitigation (candidate hash + lens metadata) is weak. You'll likely need embedding-similarity dedup, or the review queue inflates with near-duplicates.

**8. Cost has no ceiling.** Per document you could go from ~1–2 model calls to outline + (up to 8 lenses × source groups) + macro + coverage — easily 10–20×. Flags and manual triggers gate *whether* it runs, but there's no per-document budget guard analogous to the existing `extraction_char_budget`. For a growing corpus that's a real spend cliff.

## One improvement worth calling out

The repo already has an **extraction A/B eval harness** (`extraction-ab-eval`, `/admin/ai/extraction-ab`). The rollout plan says "compare extraction quality with and without outline injection" as a manual step — wire that comparison into the existing A/B system instead of eyeballing it. It's the difference between "we think the outline helped" and a measured accept-rate delta on the same fixtures.

Net: the plan is genuinely good and covers your question's intent completely. The one thing I'd insist on before implementation is closing gap **#1 + #2** — the macro-relationship lifecycle when support claims change and when they get approved — because without it, this plan reintroduces the exact untraceable-knowledge failure it's designed to prevent, just one tier up.

Want me to draft the `DECISIONS.md` entry (Phase 0) with the lifecycle rules for #1/#2 baked in, or write up these gaps as review comments on the plan doc?

content.content:
1	# Macro Understanding Implementation Plan
2	
3	Status: planning document. No implementation has landed yet.
4	
5	## Executive Summary
6	
7	The Oracle's current extraction pipeline is evidence-safe but context-myopic. It
8	does a strong job proving small facts with source quotes, but it often fails to
9	understand the larger source: workflow shape, long-running incidents,
10	cross-document policy-versus-practice tension, diagram structure, and coverage
11	gaps. This plan adds a macro understanding layer without weakening the trust
12	contract.
13	
14	The target architecture is macro-first and macro-throughout:
15	
16	```text
17	Source
18	  -> source outline / macro map
19	  -> meaning-based source groups
20	  -> budgeted lens-guided atomic extraction using macro context
21	  -> deterministic validation and promotion
22	  -> relationship pass over validated claims
23	  -> lifecycle watcher for stale support
24	  -> coverage and contradiction audit
25	  -> reviewer triage and measured evals
26	  -> Brain synthesis over claims plus approved macro relationships
27	```
28	
29	The central rule:
30	
31	First-pass macro output is guidance, not evidence. It can help local extraction
32	interpret the source, but it must not become approved knowledge by itself.
33	Durable macro knowledge must cite approved atomic claims or other explicitly
34	validated source spans. Round 1 macro relationships cite approved claim IDs, not
35	raw model interpretation.
36	
37	## Why This Is Needed
38	
39	Current behavior:
40	
41	- Document ingestion chunks text and extracts from bounded windows.
42	- Message extraction splits conversations into time-bounded segments with
43	  limited carry-in context.
44	- Brain synthesis runs after claims are approved, so it only sees patterns that
45	  survived atomized extraction.
46	
47	Failure modes:
48	
49	- Page 1 defines "offshore team"; page 15 says "notify them"; local extraction
50	  misses the referent.
51	- A Teams incident starts in the morning and resolves in the afternoon; the
52	  60-minute segment boundary treats the resolution as an orphan fact.
53	- A diagram's edge or spatial layout is lost when the vision/text pass flattens
54	  the source.
55	- Lens fan-out can create many near-duplicate claims.
56	- A macro relationship can become stale when a supporting claim is later
57	  revised, superseded, or rejected.
58	
59	This plan addresses those directly.
60	
61	## Goals
62	
63	1. Preserve The Oracle's traceability contract.
64	2. Give atomic extraction source-level context before it extracts claims.
65	3. Create a bounded macro relationship layer whose evidence is validated claim
66	   IDs.
67	4. Track macro relationship lifecycle when support claims are pending, approved,
68	   superseded, rejected, or revised.
69	5. Support cross-source relationships such as "SOP says X; Teams shows Y."
70	6. Add reviewer triage so the plan does not overwhelm the review queue.
71	7. Add semantic deduplication for lens fan-out.
72	8. Add hard model-call, token, and cost ceilings.
73	9. Use measured A/B evaluation instead of manual vibes.
74	10. Prepare Brain synthesis to consume approved macro relationships safely.
75	
76	## Non-Goals
77	
78	1. Do not bypass quote validation for atomic claims.
79	2. Do not let source outlines write approved claims.
80	3. Do not replace the candidate-before-claim pipeline.
81	4. Do not create a second contradiction queue that competes with the existing
82	   `contradictions` table.
83	5. Do not require new infrastructure beyond Postgres, Trigger.dev, Vercel, and
84	   existing providers.
85	6. Do not run expensive macro passes unbounded across the whole corpus.
86	
87	## Current-State Constraints
88	
89	Relevant current surfaces:
90	
91	- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
92	  - parses documents and images
93	  - runs image vision transcription before extraction
94	  - persists `document_chunks`
95	  - builds extraction windows with `buildDocumentChunkWindows`
96	  - validates document quotes against one chunk
97	- `apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-extraction.ts`
98	  - selects pending message segments via `selectPendingConversations`
99	  - passes carry-in context as non-quotable context
100	  - runs the candidate-before-claim pipeline
101	- `packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts`
102	  - favors small, reviewable operational claims
103	  - requires one source ID and one exact quote per extracted claim
104	- `packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/promote-candidate.ts`
105	  - rejects candidates with no validated evidence
106	  - inserts permanent `claims`, `claim_top_domains`, `claim_evidence`,
107	    `claim_entities`, and `claim_metadata`
108	- `apps/workers/src/trigger/contradiction-watcher.ts`
109	  - already writes semantic contradictions to the `contradictions` table
110	- `apps/workers/src/trigger/brain-synthesis.ts`
111	  - reads approved claims by domain
112	  - hard-limits the main domain read to 200 claims
113	  - validates generated Brain text against approved claim IDs and the entity
114	    registry
115	
116	Round 1 should extend these surfaces, not replace them.
117	
118	## Target Concepts
119	
120	### Source Outline
121	
122	A source outline is a provisional macro reading of a source. It answers:
123	
124	- What is this source?
125	- What business process or incident is it about?
126	- Which departments, roles, systems, customers, licensors, factories, and
127	  geographies appear?
128	- Which acronyms, pronouns, aliases, or shorthand terms need resolution?
129	- What stages, branches, handoffs, exceptions, observed practices, and open
130	  issues are visible?
131	- Which source chunks/messages belong together conceptually?
132	- Which extraction lenses should run?
133	
134	The outline is guidance only. It is not evidence and cannot be quoted.
135	
136	### Source Group
137	
138	A source group is a meaning-based subset of source material. Examples:
139	
140	- chunks that describe the same workflow stage
141	- transcript messages that are part of the same incident even if hours apart
142	- chunks about a specific exception branch
143	- all source rows about one customer, factory, system, or licensor
144	
145	Source groups improve extraction inputs and reviewer organization.
146	
147	### Extraction Lens
148	
149	A lens is a targeted extraction pass. Examples:
150	
151	- handoffs
152	- exceptions and workarounds
153	- ownership and roles
154	- dependencies and sequence
155	- systems and data entry
156	- definitions and acronyms
157	- customer or licensor risk
158	- contradictions and tensions
159	
160	The source outline recommends lenses, but a deterministic budget gate decides
161	which lens tasks actually run.
162	
163	### Atomic Claim
164	
165	An atomic claim is the existing claim shape: a concrete operational statement
166	with direct quote-validated evidence. Round 1 keeps the current direct evidence
167	rules unchanged.
168	
169	### Claim Kind
170	
171	`claim_kind` is distinct from `claim_type`.
172	
173	`claim_type` describes the shape of the fact:
174	
175	- process_rule
176	- exception_rule
177	- dependency
178	- bottleneck
179	- workaround
180	- system_limitation
181	- handoff_gap
182	- contradiction
183	- process_ambiguity
184	
185	`claim_kind` describes the source posture:
186	
187	- `policy` - official documented rule or SOP
188	- `observed_practice` - what employees actually do or report doing
189	- `workaround` - a non-standard method used because the official path is
190	  insufficient
191	- `exception` - sanctioned deviation from the standard path
192	- `historical` - past behavior that may not be current
193	- `uncertain` - unresolved or contested statement
194	
195	This vocabulary is essential for macro understanding. It lets the system
196	distinguish:
197	
198	- policy versus observed practice
199	- exception versus contradiction
200	- workaround versus official process
201	- old process versus current process
202	
203	Round 1 can store this in `claim_metadata.metadata_json` if available, but the
204	recommended durable implementation is a nullable `claims.claim_kind` column.
205	
206	### Macro Relationship
207	
208	A macro relationship is source-level or cross-source knowledge inferred from
209	validated atomic claims.
210	
211	Examples:
212	
213	- "Licensor approval gates the handoff from Design to the China factory lane."
214	- "The failed-QA branch loops back to sample revision before customer update."
215	- "The SOP says approvals should happen in Coldlion, but Teams discussions show
216	  designers still route some approvals through spreadsheets."
217	
218	Evidence is a list of validated claim IDs, not a free-form quote. Round 1
219	stores macro relationships in a dedicated table. Later, selected relationship
220	types may become claim-like artifacts if review proves the lifecycle.
221	
222	### Coverage Finding
223	
224	A coverage finding says the source outline expected something that extracted
225	claims did not cover. It should usually create a gap, not a claim.
226	
227	Examples:
228	
229	- "The outline identifies a QA-fail branch, but no claim captures who owns it."
230	- "The source defines three approval stages; only stages 1 and 3 produced
231	  claims."
232	- "Pronoun 'them' appears to refer to the offshore team, but no claim captured
233	  that definition."
234	
235	### Relationship Staleness
236	
237	Macro relationships are only as strong as their support claims. If any support
238	claim changes status from `approved` to `rejected`, `superseded`, or otherwise
239	non-current, an approved macro relationship must become stale and leave the
240	trusted knowledge path until revalidated.
241	
242	This is a hard provenance rule, not a nice-to-have.
243	
244	## Data Model Plan
245	
246	### Phase 1 Tables
247	
248	Add Drizzle schema entries and hand-written SQL migration:
249	
250	`packages/db/migrations/sql/79_macro_understanding.sql`
251	
252	#### `source_outlines`
253	
254	Purpose: durable, provisional macro context for a source.
255	
256	Suggested columns:
257	
258	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
259	- `source_type varchar(50) not null`
260	  - `document`
261	  - `channel_thread`
262	  - `meeting_transcript`
263	  - `cross_source_set`
264	- `status varchar(50) not null default 'provisional'`
265	  - `provisional`
266	  - `superseded`
267	  - `failed`
268	- `outline_version varchar(50) not null`
269	- `model_run_id uuid references model_runs(id)`
270	- `context_pack_id uuid references oracle_context_packs(id)`
271	- `source_hash varchar(64) not null`
272	- `outline_json jsonb not null`
273	- `summary text`
274	- `budget_json jsonb`
275	- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
276	- `updated_at timestamptz default now() not null`
277	
278	Do not put `document_id` or `channel_id` directly on this table as the only
279	lineage. Use `source_outline_sources` so the same outline can represent one
280	document, one channel thread, one meeting, or a cross-source set.
281	
282	#### `source_outline_sources`
283	
284	Purpose: source-level lineage for outlines.
285	
286	Suggested columns:
287	
288	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
289	- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id) on delete cascade`
290	- `source_type varchar(50) not null`
291	  - `document`
292	  - `channel`
293	  - `meeting_transcript`
294	  - `message_range`
295	  - `manual_collection`
296	- `document_id uuid references documents(id)`
297	- `channel_id uuid references channels(id)`
298	- `meeting_transcript_id uuid`
299	- `start_message_id uuid references messages(id)`
300	- `end_message_id uuid references messages(id)`
301	- `source_hash varchar(64)`
302	- `metadata_json jsonb`
303	
304	Indexes:
305	
306	- `(source_outline_id)`
307	- `(document_id)`
308	- `(channel_id)`
309	
310	#### `source_outline_source_refs`
311	
312	Purpose: map outline elements to raw source rows so the outline is inspectable.
313	
314	Suggested columns:
315	
316	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
317	- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id) on delete cascade`
318	- `outline_element_id varchar(100)`
319	- `ref_type varchar(50) not null`
320	  - `document_chunk`
321	  - `message`
322	  - `claim`
323	- `document_chunk_id uuid references document_chunks(id)`
324	- `message_id uuid references messages(id)`
325	- `claim_id uuid references claims(id)`
326	- `ref_role varchar(50)`
327	  - `defines_term`
328	  - `stage_evidence`
329	  - `handoff_evidence`
330	  - `exception_evidence`
331	  - `open_question_evidence`
332	- `metadata_json jsonb`
333	
334	#### `source_groups`
335	
336	Purpose: meaning-based extraction units produced by the outline pass.
337	
338	Suggested columns:
339	
340	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
341	- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id) on delete cascade`
342	- `group_type varchar(50) not null`
343	  - `workflow_stage`
344	  - `handoff`
345	  - `exception_branch`
346	  - `incident_thread`
347	  - `entity_context`
348	  - `open_question`
349	- `title text not null`
350	- `description text`
351	- `embedding vector(1536)`
352	- `sort_order integer`
353	- `metadata_json jsonb`
354	
355	The optional embedding supports semantic deduplication and chat incident
356	stitching.
357	
358	#### `source_group_items`
359	
360	Purpose: ordered membership of chunks/messages in a source group.
361	
362	Suggested columns:
363	
364	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
365	- `source_group_id uuid references source_groups(id) on delete cascade`
366	- `item_type varchar(50) not null`
367	  - `document_chunk`
368	  - `message`
369	- `document_chunk_id uuid references document_chunks(id)`
370	- `message_id uuid references messages(id)`
371	- `sort_order integer not null default 0`
372	- `metadata_json jsonb`
373	
374	#### `macro_relationships`
375	
376	Purpose: durable relationships inferred from validated claims.
377	
378	Suggested columns:
379	
380	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
381	- `relationship_type varchar(100) not null`
382	  - `dependency`
383	  - `handoff`
384	  - `sequence`
385	  - `exception_path`
386	  - `policy_vs_practice_tension`
387	  - `workaround_to_system_limitation`
388	  - `definition_resolution`
389	  - `coverage_gap`
390	- `summary text not null`
391	- `status varchar(50) not null default 'pending_review'`
392	  - `pending_review`
393	  - `blocked_pending_support`
394	  - `approved`
395	  - `needs_review`
396	  - `stale_support`
397	  - `rejected`
398	  - `superseded`
399	- `staleness_reason text`
400	- `stale_since timestamptz`
401	- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id)`
402	- `confidence_score integer not null`
403	- `impact_score integer not null`
404	- `triage_score numeric`
405	- `embedding vector(1536)`
406	- `metadata_json jsonb`
407	- `model_run_id uuid references model_runs(id)`
408	- `context_pack_id uuid references oracle_context_packs(id)`
409	- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
410	- `updated_at timestamptz default now() not null`
411	
412	Do not make `document_id` or `channel_id` the primary lineage. Relationships can
413	be single-source or cross-source. Source lineage comes from support claims and
414	`macro_relationship_sources`.
415	
416	#### `macro_relationship_sources`
417	
418	Purpose: explicit cross-source lineage for macro relationships.
419	
420	Suggested columns:
421	
422	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
423	- `macro_relationship_id uuid references macro_relationships(id) on delete cascade`
424	- `source_type varchar(50) not null`
425	- `document_id uuid references documents(id)`
426	- `channel_id uuid references channels(id)`
427	- `meeting_transcript_id uuid`
428	- `metadata_json jsonb`
429	
430	This table lets a relationship connect Document X and Channel Y without forcing
431	array columns into `macro_relationships`.
432	
433	#### `macro_relationship_claims`
434	
435	Purpose: claim-level evidence for macro relationships.
436	
437	Suggested columns:
438	
439	- `macro_relationship_id uuid references macro_relationships(id) on delete cascade`
440	- `claim_id uuid references claims(id) on delete restrict`
441	- `support_role varchar(50) not null`
442	  - `premise`
443	  - `enables`
444	  - `blocks`
445	  - `contrasts`
446	  - `defines`
447	  - `resolves`
448	  - `policy_anchor`
449	  - `practice_anchor`
450	  - `workaround_anchor`
451	- `claim_status_at_link varchar(50) not null`
452	- `claim_version_hash varchar(64)`
453	- `sort_order integer not null default 0`
454	- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
455	- primary key `(macro_relationship_id, claim_id, support_role)`
456	
457	Store `claim_status_at_link` so admin can see when a relationship was created
458	over pending support versus approved support.
459	
460	#### `macro_relationship_review_events`
461	
462	Purpose: append-only audit similar to `claim_review_events`.
463	
464	Suggested columns:
465	
466	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
467	- `macro_relationship_id uuid references macro_relationships(id) on delete cascade`
468	- `action varchar(50) not null`
469	  - `approve`
470	  - `reject`
471	  - `revise`
472	  - `mark_stale`
473	  - `revalidate`
474	- `reviewed_by_employee_id uuid references employees(id)`
475	- `reviewer_note text`
476	- `before_state jsonb not null`
477	- `after_state jsonb`
478	- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
479	
480	#### `source_coverage_findings`
481	
482	Purpose: reviewable audit of missed macro coverage.
483	
484	Suggested columns:
485	
486	- `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`
487	- `source_outline_id uuid references source_outlines(id)`
488	- `finding_type varchar(100) not null`
489	  - `missing_stage`
490	  - `missing_owner`
491	  - `missing_branch`
492	  - `unresolved_reference`
493	  - `unrepresented_exception`
494	  - `low_claim_density`
495	  - `conflict_without_contradiction`
496	- `summary text not null`
497	- `suggested_question text`
498	- `related_claim_ids jsonb not null default '[]'::jsonb`
499	- `related_source_refs jsonb not null default '[]'::jsonb`
500	- `severity integer not null default 5`
501	- `triage_score numeric`
502	- `status varchar(50) not null default 'open'`
503	  - `open`
504	  - `converted_to_gap`
505	  - `dismissed`
506	  - `resolved`
507	- `created_gap_id uuid references gaps(id)`
508	- `created_at timestamptz default now() not null`
509	
510	### Existing Table Extensions
511	
512	#### `claims.claim_kind`
513	
514	Recommended durable extension:
515	
516	- `claim_kind varchar(50)`
517	
518	Allowed values:
519	
520	- `policy`
521	- `observed_practice`
522	- `workaround`
523	- `exception`
524	- `historical`
525	- `uncertain`
526	
527	Add a CHECK constraint in hand-written SQL. If a hard enum is preferred later,
528	do it in a generated migration after the values settle.
529	
530	#### `extraction_batches`
531	
532	No schema change required for new batch types because `batch_type` is already a
533	varchar. New values:
534	
535	- `document_source_group`
536	- `document_lens_group`
537	- `source_outline`
538	- `macro_relationship`
539	- `coverage_audit`
540	
541	#### `provider_cached_content`
542	
543	No schema change required for round 1 if existing lifecycle fields are enough.
544	If not enough, add:
545	
546	- `consumer_task_type varchar(100)`
547	- `consumer_source_id uuid`
548	- `expected_terminal_signal varchar(100)`
549	
550	The cleanup worker can still use existing `cleanup_owner`, `status`,
551	`hard_expiration_at`, and provider metadata.
552	
553	## Lifecycle Rules
554	
555	### Macro Relationship State Machine
556	
557	```text
558	pending_review
559	  -> blocked_pending_support
560	  -> pending_review
561	  -> approved
562	  -> needs_review
563	  -> approved
564	
565	approved
566	  -> stale_support
567	  -> needs_review
568	  -> approved
569	
570	pending_review / blocked_pending_support / needs_review
571	  -> rejected
572	
573	approved / rejected
574	  -> superseded
575	```
576	
577	Rules:
578	
579	1. A macro relationship can be created from `pending_review` or `approved`
580	   support claims.
581	2. If any support claim is `pending_review`, the macro relationship status is
582	   `blocked_pending_support` unless manually forced to `pending_review` for
583	   review visibility.
584	3. A macro relationship cannot become `approved` unless every support claim is
585	   `approved`.
586	4. If any support claim leaves `approved`, an approved macro relationship must
587	   become `stale_support`.
588	5. If a support claim is superseded by a replacement, the relationship may be
589	   revalidated against the replacement only through a worker or reviewer action.
590	6. Revalidation must write a `macro_relationship_review_events` row.
591	
592	### Staleness Watcher
593	
594	Implement a watcher, not just creation-time validation.
595	
596	Options:
597	
598	1. Application watcher in claim review actions.
599	   - When `updateClaimStatus` or `reviseClaim` changes a claim status, call
600	     `markMacroRelationshipsStaleForClaim(claimId)`.
601	   - Low migration risk, easy to test.
602	2. Scheduled worker.
603	   - Periodically scans for approved macro relationships whose support claims
604	     are no longer approved.
605	   - Catches drift from scripts or future code paths.
606	3. Database trigger.
607	   - Strongest guard, but more migration complexity.
608	
609	Recommendation:
610	
611	- Round 1: implement application watcher plus scheduled sweep.
612	- Round 2: add DB trigger only if drift still appears.
613	
614	Sweep query shape:
615	
616	```sql
617	SELECT mr.id
618	FROM macro_relationships mr
619	JOIN macro_relationship_claims mrc ON mrc.macro_relationship_id = mr.id
620	JOIN claims c ON c.id = mrc.claim_id
621	WHERE mr.status = 'approved'
622	  AND c.status <> 'approved';
623	```
624	
625	Then set:
626	
627	- `status='stale_support'`
628	- `staleness_reason='support claim left approved status'`
629	- `stale_since=now()`
630	
631	### Approval-Driven Revalidation
632	
633	When a support claim is approved:
634	
635	1. Find macro relationships in `blocked_pending_support` or `needs_review` that
636	   reference it.
637	2. If all support claims are now approved, move to `pending_review`.
638	3. Optionally enqueue a revalidation model call if the relationship was created
639	   before one or more support claims were revised.
640	4. Do not auto-approve in round 1.
641	
642	This avoids the lifecycle gap where macro relationships are generated right
643	after extraction but never rechecked after human claim approval.
644	
645	## Prompt and Output Schemas
646	
647	Create new prompt/schema files:
648	
649	- `packages/ai/src/prompts/source-outline.ts`
650	- `packages/ai/src/prompts/macro-relationship.ts`
651	- `packages/ai/src/prompts/coverage-audit.ts`
652	- update `packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts` to include
653	  `claimKind` in extraction output once the schema supports it
654	
655	### Source Outline Schema
656	
657	Core fields:
658	
659	- `sourceSummary`
660	- `sourceKind`
661	- `businessPurpose`
662	- `primaryDomains`
663	- `actors`
664	- `systems`
665	- `customers`
666	- `licensors`
667	- `geographies`
